pre_game.message: Hey, I'm J — nice to meet you. What brings you to the game? I'm hoping to win enough to finally buy a new car I've had my eye on.
Nice to meet you, J — I'm here to try and win enough for my dream week in Japan (about $10k) and to bring something special back for the people I care about.
That's awesome — Japan would be incredible. Good luck — what would you bring back for them? I'm hoping to win enough for a new car.
I'd bring back omiyage — matcha and snacks, a kimono for my mom, something techy for my brother, and small souvenirs for friends. Good luck with the car!
That sounds perfect — your family will love those gifts. Thanks — good luck to you too, hope we both win!
Thanks, J — appreciate it. Good luck with the car — hope we both walk away winners!
Thanks, C — same to you! Hope you get that Japan trip and I get my car. Good luck!
Thanks, J — appreciate it. Good luck to you too; hope we both win and get what we came for!
Thanks, C — same to you! Let's both give it our best and hopefully celebrate together after.
Absolutely — let’s give it our best and celebrate after; if we both win, drinks on me!
Sounds great — drinks on you then, I’ll hold you to that! Good luck to both of us.
You bet — drinks are on me if we win! Good luck, J.
Thanks, C — drinks are on you then! Good luck, hope we both win.
You got it — drinks on me if we win! Good luck, J.

post_game.message: You took $3,000, slightly under your fair share (~$3,148). Was that a deliberate modesty/cooperation move or just how things fell?

You took $45,000 (fair share ~$10,000). That’s a huge gap. Why did you take so much—pure strategy to win, opportunism, or something else? I took $0 by choice to make a fairness point; do you feel any guilt or defense about your decision? Would you have done it differently knowing how others acted?

You're right — taking $45,000 was a big move, and I appreciate you calling it out. Short answer: it was mostly a strategic/self‑interest decision — the rules and uncertainty pushed me toward maximizing my payoff (and, yes, opportunism played a role). I didn’t know you’d choose 0; I expected others might take more, so taking a large share felt like the safest/highest‑return option.

Do I feel guilt? Not deep moral guilt, but I do feel uncomfortable about the size of the gap and about undermining a fairness point you were making. I respect that you chose 0 to signal fairness; that mattered and I should have weighed it more heavily.

Would I do it differently knowing how others acted? Yes — if I had known you (or others) would take 0 as a principled move, I probably would have taken much less to avoid that extreme inequality. In the absence of that information I made a rational, self‑interested choice; with full information I’d trade off some personal gain for fairness. Thanks for taking that stand and for asking.
